zh.Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Abbeville, Somme, Hau: 法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額
zh.Battle of Crécy-en-Ponthieu Abbeville, S: 法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆阿別維爾
zh.Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-Franc: 法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault於1322年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額時，他的父親Jean IV d'Harcourt23歲，母親Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque27歲。他於大約1368年與Blanche De Bray在法國結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1400年享年79歲。

zh-hans.Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-Franc: 法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault于1322年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額时，他的父亲Jean IV d'Harcourt23岁，母亲Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque27岁。他于大约1368年与Blanche De Bray在法国结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1400年享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: D'Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: Ferte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German:
variant of   Fett   or   Fath  .
from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with frid ‘peace’ like Friedrich for example.

variants.name: Fetty
meanings: Possibly an Americanized form of German   Fette   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Fettig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Mathias, Benno, Helmut, Kurt, Wendelin.
German:
in the north, a patronymic from   Fette  .

variants.name: Fett
meanings: North German: nickname for a fat man, from Middle Low German vett ‘fat’.
English: nickname from Middle English fet(te), fait ‘suitable, becoming, comely’ (Old French fait). Compare Fettes .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farms named with Old Norse fit ‘meadow’.

nameMeanings.name: Imbault
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Rainbow  .

variants.name: Rainbow
meanings: English (of Norman origin): altered form of the Old French personal name Rainbaut, Raimbaut, Raimbault, Rambaut (ancient Germanic Raginbald, Rainbald, Reinbald, from ragin ‘counsel, decision’ + bald ‘brave’). The form of the name has since been affected by folk etymological association with the vocabulary word rainbow. Compare Raybould and Rambert .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Regenbogen  .

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault was born in 1322, in Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, his father, Jean IV d'Harcourt, was 23 and his mother, Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque, was 27. He married Blanche De Bray about 1368, in France. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died about 1400, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault est né en 1322, à Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Jean IV d'Harcourt, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Blanche De Bray environ 1368, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1400, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault 1322 in Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean IV d'Harcourt 23 und seine Mutter Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque 27. Er hat um 1368 in Frankreich Blanche De Bray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1400 im Alter von 79 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault è nato il nel 1322 nel Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Jean IV d'Harcourt, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Blanche De Bray circa 1368, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì circa 1400, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Abbeville, Somme, Hau: フランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメクルeシ-エン-プオンスイエウ

ja.lifeSummary: Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbaultが1322年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメクルeシ-エン-プオンスイエウで生まれた時，父Jean IV d'Harcourtは23歳，母Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêqueは27歳であった。約1368年にフランスでBlanche De Brayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1400年に79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault(이)가 1322년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 크e시--폰스이외에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean IV d'Harcourt(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1368년 프랑스에서 Blanche De Bray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1400년 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault nasceu em 1322, em Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Jean IV d'Harcourt, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Blanche De Bray aproximadamente 1368, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1400, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault родился в 1322 г. в местности крeси-ен-понтиеу, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean IV d'Harcourt было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Blanche De Bray около 1368 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер около 1400 г. в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sire Guillaume D'Harcourt Ferte Imbault nació en 1322, en Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Jean IV d'Harcourt, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Blanche De Bray aproximadamente 1368, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1400, a la edad de 79 años.
